Overview. At Trane Technologies, we Challenge Possible. Our brands \u2013 including Trane\u00AE and Thermo King\u00AE \u2013 create access to cooling and comfort in buildings and homes, transport and protect food and perishables, connect customers to elevated performance with less environmental impact, and lead innovations for a more sustainable world. We boldly challenge what\u2019s possible for a more sustainable future. BrainBox AI (BBAI), a business of Trane Technologies, utilizes self-adapted artificial intelligence technology to proactively optimize the energy consumption of one of the largest climate change contributors: Buildings. Our AI engine supports a self-operating building that requires no human intervention. Using deep learning, cloud-based computing, and our proprietary process, our solution autonomously optimizes existing Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) control systems for maximum impact on energy consumption. We are seeking an AI Intern \u2013 Product Management for Workflow Automation for our Montreal, QC location. The AI Intern \u2013 Product Management will own internal voice of the customer, prioritization, and scoping for automation to be used within the BrainBox AI enterprise. Work with development resources to define requirements, acceptance criteria, and deliver automation that directly contributes and supports business goals. The ideal candidate is detail\u2011oriented, eager to learn, and thrives in a fast\u2011paced, collaborative environment. Key Responsibilities. Gather and document the internal voice of the customer by gathering feedback, understanding needs, and translating pain points into actionable automation opportunities. Lead prioritization and scoping of workflow automation initiatives based on business value, customer impact, and organizational goals. Partner with development teams to define detailed requirements, acceptance criteria, and scope for automation features. Manage and communicate progress across automation initiatives, ensuring alignment with business priorities and timelines. Support the creation of process documentation, user stories, and functional specifications. Validate and test automation outputs to ensure they meet requirements and deliver intended business value. Collaborate with stakeholders across teams to identify improvements to current processes and propose automation\u2011driven solutions. Contribute to building a scalable product management framework for workflow automation within the BrainBox AI enterprise. Qualifications. Currently pursuing a degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Data Science, Business, Product Management, or a related field. Strong analytical and problem\u2011solving skills, with the ability to break down complex processes. Excellent communication skills, with the ability to gather insights from diverse stakeholders and translate them into clear requirements. Interest in workflow automation, AI\u2011enabled solutions, and process optimization. Experience with project management or product management methodologies is a plus. Familiarity with tools such as Jira, Azure DevOps, Asana, or similar platforms is helpful. What You Will Gain. Hands\u2011on experience shaping the roadmap for workflow automation in a leading AI\u2011driven organization. Direct exposure to cross\u2011functional collaboration with engineering, product, and business teams. Practical experience in defining requirements and acceptance criteria for automation solutions. An opportunity to drive efficiency and innovation within real-world enterprise workflows. Additional Information. Internship Duration: Fixed-term internship with flexible start dates based on academic schedules. Location: Onsite in Montreal. Compensation: Competitive hourly rate. This role offers valuable exposure to product commercialization processes, cross-functional teamwork, and the rapidly growing field of AI solutions. Candidates from diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply. Language Requirements. French-English bilingualism is required. In addition to fluency in French, successful candidates must have full professional proficiency in English in order to support and collaborate with English-speaking clients, colleagues and/or various stakeholders. Knowledge/Skills. Familiarity with HVAC equipment, building automation, or energy management systems is an asset. Proficient with Excel and data analysis tools; exposure to programming (Python, R, SQL) is an advantage. Excellent written and verbal communication skills (French and English strongly preferred for the Montreal market). Strong interpersonal skills and collaborative mindset. Demonstrated self-motivation, problem-solving, and critical thinking abilities. Key Competencies. Analytical and detail-oriented: Able to synthesize complex data and deliver actionable insights. Innovative: Continuously seeking new ways to improve systems, processes, and results. Customer-focused: Understand customer needs and strive to exceed expectations. Collaborative: Works well in cross-functional and diverse team environments. Driven and Results-Oriented: Takes initiative to deliver project objectives with quality and efficiency.
